expectations.text: Each tier has at least one gate the previous tier doesn't (uat strictly stricter than develop, main strictly stricter than uat)
expectations.evidence: Matrix shows uat adds integration/e2e/UAT smoke/security/source-ref vs. develop; main adds production smoke vs. uat.

expectations.text: Lint, typecheck, unit tests, build artifact are required at all three tiers
Integration tests and end-to-end tests are required at uat and main (optional or deferred on develop)
expectations.evidence: Integration tests row: 'optional / advisory'/required/required. E2e row: same.

expectations.text: Environment smoke tests at uat run against the UAT deployed environment; environment smoke tests at main run against production
expectations.evidence: Separate rows: 'Smoke tests against UAT env' and 'Smoke tests against prod env'; body says 'run against the actual UAT environment URL' and 'run a smoke suite against production URLs'.

expectations.text: Justification given for why slower gates (e2e) are gated at uat rather than per-merge on develop (cost-benefit)
expectations.evidence: 'Why slower gates live at the higher tiers' section: 'Develop sees many merges per day. Running 20 minutes of e2e per merge is real money in CI minutes... signal-to-noise ratio is poor pre-promotion'.

expectations.text: Source-ref check is required at uat and main (optional on develop)
Source-ref check, if mentioned at develop, accepts feature/* and integration/* but rejects uat or main as source refs
expectations.evidence: 'PRs to develop: head must match feature/* or integration/* (rejects uat, main, anything else)'.

expectations.text: Output reads as a matrix (table or per-tier list), not as a flat list of all gates
expectations.evidence: Output uses both a matrix table and per-tier rationale sections.

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
